response: Originating in the 1960s as a US Department of Defense initiative, the internet evolved from the 1969 ARPANET demonstration and the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP protocols into a foundational global network. The 1989 invention of the World Wide Web and the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser democratized access, fueling the dot-com boom, the mid-2000s social media expansion, and the mobile revolution following the 2007 iPhone release. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people worldwide, serving as an indispensable infrastructure that fundamentally drives modern commerce, communication, and daily life.
